info.name: Baton Teleport - Connector API Calls
info.description:
Postman collection documenting all Teleport API calls made by the baton-teleport connector.

**Important:** The connector uses Teleport's native Go gRPC client, not REST. However, Teleport exposes equivalent HTTP endpoints through its web proxy API. This collection uses those HTTP endpoints.

**Authentication:** Teleport's web API uses bearer tokens obtained from a web session. Use the "Login" request first to get a session token, then it will be used automatically by other requests.

**Setup:**
1. Set the `proxy_address` variable to your Teleport cluster (e.g., `yourname.teleport.sh`)
2. Run the "Login" request with your credentials
3. The bearer token will be automatically saved for subsequent requests

variable.description: Your Teleport proxy address (without port or protocol)
Your Teleport cluster name (usually same as proxy address)
Bearer token from web session (auto-populated by Login request)

item.name: Authentication
item.description: Authenticate to get a web session bearer token. Run the Login request first before any other requests.

request.description: Check if the Teleport proxy is reachable. Returns cluster info, auth type, and server version. No authentication required.

Lightweight cluster discovery endpoint. Returns proxy address, auth preferences, and cluster name. No authentication required.

**Connector equivalent:** Used implicitly during client connection setup.

Create a web session by logging in with username, password, and OTP code.

The `second_factor_token` is the 6-digit OTP code from your authenticator app.

The response contains a bearer token that will be automatically saved and used by other requests.

**Note:** The connector does NOT use this endpoint. It authenticates via mTLS using the identity file (auth.pem). This endpoint is provided so you can authenticate Postman requests.

item.name: Users
item.description:
User management operations. The connector syncs all users and supports account creation and deletion.

**Connector methods:**
- `List()` → GetUsers
- `CreateAccount()` → CreateUser + CreateResetPasswordToken
- `Delete()` → GetUser + DeleteUser

request.description:
List all users in the Teleport cluster.

**Connector method:** `userBuilder.List()`
**Go SDK call:** `client.GetUsers(ctx, false)`

Returns all users with their metadata, roles, traits, and status (locked/unlocked).

**Fields used by connector:**
- `name` → Resource ID and display name
- `metadata.revision` → User profile ID
- `status.is_locked` → User enabled/disabled status
- `roles` → Used for grant generation
- `spec.traits` → User traits
- `IsBot()` → Account type (service vs human)

Get a specific user by username.

**Connector method:** `userBuilder.Delete()` (to verify user exists and is local before deletion)
**Go SDK call:** `client.GetUser(ctx, username, false)`

Also used in `roleBuilder.Grant()` and `roleBuilder.Revoke()` to fetch the user before modifying their roles.

Create a new local user in Teleport.

**Connector method:** `userBuilder.CreateAccount()`
**Go SDK call:** `client.CreateUser(ctx, user)`

The connector creates users with:
- `kind`: "user"
- `version`: "v2"
- `metadata.name`: cleaned username
- `spec.roles`: ["access"] (default) or specified role
- `spec.traits.logins`: [username]

**Important:** After creating the user, the connector also creates a password reset token (see next request).

Update an existing user. Used by the connector to add or remove roles.

**Connector methods:**
- `roleBuilder.Grant()` → Adds a role to user's role list
- `roleBuilder.Revoke()` → Removes a role from user's role list

**Go SDK call:** `client.UpdateUser(ctx, user)`

**Grant flow:**
1. GetUser(username)
2. Add role name to user's roles list
3. Add username to user's logins
4. UpdateUser(user)

**Revoke flow:**
1. GetUser(username)
2. Remove role name from user's roles list
3. UpdateUser(user)

Delete a local user from Teleport.

**Connector method:** `userBuilder.Delete()`
**Go SDK call:** `client.DeleteUser(ctx, username)`

**Important:** The connector first checks if the user is a local user (not SSO/federated). Only local users can be deleted via the connector.

Create a password reset token for a user. Returns a URL the user can visit to set their password.

**Connector method:** `userBuilder.CreateAccount()` (called after creating the user)
**Go SDK call:** `client.CreateResetPasswordToken(ctx, &proto.CreateResetPasswordTokenRequest{...})`

The connector creates tokens with a 24-hour TTL. The returned URL is sent to a ConductorOne vault so the new user can configure their password.

**Note:** Teleport does not allow auto-generating passwords for new users due to security rules. This is why the connector returns a reset link instead.

item.name: Roles
item.description:
Role management operations. The connector syncs all roles and supports granting/revoking role membership.

**Connector methods:**
- `List()` → GetRoles
- `Entitlements()` → Returns "member" entitlement per role
- `Grants()` → GetUsers, then checks each user's roles
- `Grant()` → GetUser + UpdateUser (add role)
- `Revoke()` → GetUser + UpdateUser (remove role)

request.description:
List all roles in the Teleport cluster.

**Connector method:** `roleBuilder.List()`
**Go SDK call:** `client.GetRoles(ctx)`

Returns all roles with their metadata, allow/deny rules, and options.

**Fields used by connector:**
- `metadata.name` → Resource ID and display name
- `metadata.revision` → Role profile ID
- `metadata.description` → Role description in profile

Get a specific role by name.

**Note:** Not directly used by the connector, but useful for inspecting role details (allow/deny rules, node labels, etc.).

item.name: Nodes
item.description:
Node (server) listing. The connector syncs nodes with pagination.

**Connector methods:**
- `List()` → GetResources (paginated)
- `Grants()` → Not implemented (TODO: associate nodes with roles)

request.description:
List all nodes (servers) in the cluster. Supports pagination via `startKey`.

**Connector method:** `nodeBuilder.List()`
**Go SDK call:** `client.GetResources(ctx, &proto.ListResourcesRequest{ResourceType: "node", StartKey: token})`

**Fields used by connector:**
- `node.revision` → Node ID
- `node.hostname` → Node name
- `node.namespace` → Node namespace

**Pagination:** Pass the `nextKey` from the response as `startKey` in the next request. Empty `nextKey` means no more pages.

item.name: Applications
item.description:
Application listing. The connector syncs registered applications.

**Connector methods:**
- `List()` → GetApps
- `Grants()` → Not implemented (returns empty)

request.description:
List all registered applications in the cluster.

**Connector method:** `appBuilder.List()`
**Go SDK call:** `client.GetApps(ctx)`

**Fields used by connector:**
- `metadata.revision` → App ID
- `name` → App name

item.name: Databases
item.description:
Database listing. The connector syncs registered databases.

**Connector methods:**
- `List()` → GetDatabases
- `Grants()` → Not implemented (returns empty)

request.description:
List all registered databases in the cluster.

**Connector method:** `dbBuilder.List()`
**Go SDK call:** `client.GetDatabases(ctx)`

**Fields used by connector:**
- `revision` → Database ID
- `name` → Database name

item.name: Audit Events
item.description:
Audit event endpoints for querying cluster activity. Useful for implementing event feeds (usage tracking, audit logs).

**Go SDK call:** `client.SearchEvents(ctx, from, to, namespace, eventTypes, limit, order, startKey)`

query.description: Maximum number of events to return

request.description:
Search all audit events in a time range.

**Go SDK call:** `client.SearchEvents(ctx, from, to, apidefaults.Namespace, nil, limit, types.EventOrderDescending, startKey)`

**Common event types returned:**
- `user.login` - User login (success/failure, MFA device)
- `cert.create` - Certificate creation (identity, roles, IP)
- `mfa_auth_challenge.create` - MFA challenge issued
- `user.create` - User account created
- `role.created` - Role created
- `user.update` - User updated
- `session.start` / `session.end` - SSH sessions

**Pagination:** Use the `startKey` from the response to get the next page.

query.description: Maximum number of events to return

request.description:
Search only user login events. Useful for usage tracking / last login.

**Go SDK call:** `client.SearchEvents(ctx, from, to, apidefaults.Namespace, []string{"user.login"}, limit, order, startKey)`

**Response fields:**
- `user` - Who logged in
- `success` - Login success/failure
- `method` - Auth method (local, github, saml, oidc)
- `mfa_device` - MFA device used (name, UUID, type)
- `addr.remote` - Client IP address
- `user_agent` - Client user agent

query.description: Maximum number of events to return

request.description:
Search for access-related change events (user/role CRUD). Useful for audit log event feeds.

**Go SDK call:** `client.SearchEvents(ctx, from, to, ns, []string{"user.create", "user.update", "user.delete", "role.created", "role.deleted"}, limit, order, startKey)`

**Full list of event types:** https://goteleport.com/docs/reference/audit-events/

item.name: Unified Resources
item.description: Teleport's unified resource listing endpoint. Not used directly by the connector, but useful for exploring all resources at once.

query.description: Maximum number of resources to return
Filter by resource type: node, app, db, kube_cluster, windows_desktop

request.description:
List all infrastructure resources across types (nodes, apps, databases, Kubernetes clusters, Windows desktops).

Useful for getting a complete view of what the connector will sync.

Filter by type using the `kind` parameter:
- `node` - Servers
- `app` - Applications
- `db` - Databases
- `kube_cluster` - Kubernetes clusters
- `windows_desktop` - Windows desktops
